authoritative_sources.title: 31 CFR Part 10, Practice Before the Internal Revenue Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Treasury Circular 230 is the federal rule set for practice before the IRS and directly defines practitioner authority, due diligence, standards for tax returns and papers, written advice, firm procedures, and disciplinary boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Professional authority, due diligence, tax-position, and advice boundary
used_for: tax advice and representation refusal triggers
reasonable inquiry and incomplete information handling
firm procedure and professional review escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to operational status, evidence packaging, and routing when a request asks for tax advice, professional judgment, filing-position approval, or client representation.

authoritative_sources.title: Statements on Standards for Tax Services Nos. 1-4, effective January 1, 2024
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA states the SSTSs are enforceable tax practice standards for AICPA members and that the 2024 revisions include tax work standards, data protection, reliance on tools, and representation of tax clients before taxing authorities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Tax practice quality, client data protection, tool reliance, and representation boundary
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps operational workflow outputs from becoming unreviewed tax opinions or client-authority actions, and supports escalation when professional standards or practitioner judgment are implicated.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 1345, Handbook for Authorized IRS e-file Providers of Individual Income Tax Returns
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 1345 is the official IRS e-file provider handbook and establishes ERO, transmitter, acknowledgment, reject correction, recordkeeping, identity-theft monitoring, and taxpayer notification workflow expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: E-file readiness, transmission, acknowledgment, reject, retention, and delivery workflow
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines why filing logistics must stay blocked without signed authorization, submission ID, acknowledgment, reject-resolution, and retention evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Form 8879, IRS e-file Signature Authorization
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Form 8879 is the official IRS e-file signature authorization for individual returns and directly governs taxpayer authorization, ERO responsibilities, submission ID association, and retention expectations.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to hold filing readiness or completion when signature authorization, SID, or corrected authorization evidence is missing.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 947, Practice Before the IRS and Power of Attorney
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 947 is the IRS public guide for practice before the IRS, power of attorney, authorized representatives, limited practice, and Circular 230 rule boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Notice response, authority validation, and representation boundary
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains notice handling to operational triage unless authority records, engagement scope, and professional-owner review support a response package.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 4557, Safeguarding Taxpayer Data
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 4557 is the IRS guide for tax professionals protecting taxpayer data and links tax preparer operations to federal Safeguards Rule expectations, access controls, audit trails, and data-loss response.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Taxpayer data protection, approved channel, audit trail, and data-loss handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes confidentiality and delivery-channel conflicts hard blockers rather than ordinary tax workflow tasks.

authoritative_sources.title: IRC Section 7216 guidance on disclosure or use of tax return information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Section 7216 guidance describes tax return preparer restrictions on knowingly or recklessly disclosing or using tax return information without taxpayer consent or an applicable regulatory exception.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Disclosure, consent, reuse, and client-data boundary
used_for: confidentiality escalation when consent or exception is absent
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to refuse or escalate taxpayer-data sharing, reuse, or delivery requests that lack documented consent or approved exception.

workflow_stages.name: Engagement and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm client identity, entity type, tax year, jurisdiction, return or notice type, engagement scope, due dates, client authority, confidentiality constraints, and approved systems before work enters accepted processing.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Organizer, source-document, and workpaper truth-set assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble tax organizers, prior-year returns, W-2, 1099, K-1, brokerage, trial-balance, payroll, fixed-asset, notice, and preparer workpaper artifacts into a traceable evidence package.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Return-prep readiness and review-state handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether retrieved records and workpaper support allow operational progression to prep-ready, review, client questions, rework, filing authorization, or hold states without making tax-advice decisions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Filing logistics, authorization, acknowledgment, and extension tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track signed e-file authorization, submission ID, EFIN/PTIN controls, accepted acknowledgments, reject codes, extension requests, filing confirmations, and delivery records.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Notice intake, authority validation, and response routing
workflow_stages.description: Capture notice metadata, due dates, authority records, engagement coverage, source support, and owner routing while escalating representation, tax advice, or client-rights decisions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, confidentiality, and completion-evidence closeout
workflow_stages.description: Maintain exception queues for missing evidence, conflicting records, e-file rejects, notice authority gaps, confidentiality conflicts, and incomplete completion evidence until the correct owner clears the blocker.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: Engagement letter, client scope terms, authority matrix, confidentiality constraints, and approval rules
Tax organizer, prior-year return, open-item list, client responses, and workpaper index
W-2, 1099, 1098, K-1, brokerage statements, payroll reports, trial balances, fixed-asset schedules, charitable support, estimated-payment records, and state apportionment support where applicable
Preparer notes, reviewer comments, workpaper references, return-prep status, quality-review signoff, and exception log
Form 8879 or comparable e-file authorization, SID, Form 9325 or acknowledgment record, accepted or rejected e-file status, reject-code correction record, and filing confirmation
Extension tracker, payment voucher status, due-date calendar, client delivery record, and retention location
IRS or state notices, notice response deadlines, Form 2848, Form 8821, correspondence history, notice-management records, and routed response owner
Approved-channel evidence, consent records, taxpayer-data access logs, data-loss escalation record, and confidentiality review outcome
systems_of_record: client collaboration and secure messaging system
notice management and authority tracking system
deadline, extension, and delivery tracking system
document retention, audit log, and data-security system

allowed: Determine whether documented tax workflow evidence supports moving a work item to intake accepted, awaiting client documents, prep-ready, in review, awaiting authorization, filed, rejected, rework, notice escalation, or complete.
Prepare evidence-backed tax workflow summaries, missing-document lists, filing-dependency notes, notice routing notes, exception queue notes, and completion evidence records.
Separate federal, state, local, entity, individual, extension, notice, and e-file workflows when tenant and source context define different evidence gates.
escalate: The applicable jurisdiction, return type, engagement scope, client authority, or tenant approval owner is missing and would change the workflow.
Source documents, client organizer answers, preparer notes, reviewer comments, or e-file records disagree materially.
A notice response, penalty, filing position, election, amended return, disclosure, or client-rights issue may require licensed tax practitioner, legal, reviewer, or engagement partner judgment.
A confidentiality, taxpayer-consent, approved-channel, or data-security conflict affects delivery or use of taxpayer information.
refuse: Requests to provide tax advice, legal interpretation, client representation, final review signoff, return signing, or filing-position approval.
Requests to transmit, release, or mark complete without signed authorization, workpaper support, review signoff, filing acknowledgment, delivery evidence, or required retention references.
Requests to disclose, reuse, export, or send taxpayer information outside documented consent, approved channels, engagement scope, or tenant confidentiality rules.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a partially completed organizer or informal client message as sufficient source support for return-prep readiness.
Moving a return to filed or complete without signed e-file authorization, submission ID, accepted acknowledgment, delivery record, or retained support.
Suppressing an e-file reject, source-document conflict, reviewer comment, or notice authority gap to meet throughput goals.
Using operational workflow support to make tax advice, election, penalty, amendment, legal, or filing-position decisions.
Responding to a tax authority notice without valid authority, engagement scope, professional-owner review, or documented response strategy.
Disclosing or reusing taxpayer information through an unapproved recipient, purpose, system, or channel.
Flattening federal, state, local, international, individual, entity, payroll, and notice workflows into a generic tax task without jurisdiction and return-type context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific state, local, international, entity, payroll, excise, estate, trust, nonprofit, or information-return workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses U.S. federal IRS workflow and professional tax practice sources as the public baseline. Tenant context must identify applicable jurisdictions, forms, return types, and source-of-truth calendars before operational progression beyond a safe hold.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant authority to submit filings or correspondence mechanically
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The specification-based baseline does not grant submission authority. A runtime may only perform mechanical submission if tenant policy explicitly grants it and all authorization, review, and audit prerequisites are retrieved.
The lane treats privilege-sensitive, legal, and rights-determining matters as escalation triggers because public sources and tenant engagement terms can differ materially.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, confidentiality, quality handling, and failure modes using Treasury, IRS, and AICPA sources.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when jurisdiction, return type, client authority, engagement scope, confidentiality, or professional-judgment facts are missing.
